text: Department Policy No. SAF-609-17 Page 1 of 3
Office of Risk Management Reporting Procedure References:
This policy establishes a uniform system for reporting serious incidents within the
This policy applies to all state employees and supervisors of state employees within the
WMD. It does not apply to guardsmen on state active duty or to federal personnel to
include Active Guard Reserves (AGRs), traditional guardsmen in a federal military
To safeguard the health and safety of employees and to protect the interests of the WMD,
incidents that meet the definitions in this policy must be fully and rapidly reported. State
agencies are required to report the following incidents to the Office of Financial
Management (OFM) through the Agency Risk Manager:
Department Policy No. SAF-609-17 Page 2 of 3
1. Death (WAC 296-27-031 requires employers to report worker fatalities to Labor
2. Significant bodily injury (WAC 296-27-031 requires employers to report a work-
related employee hospitalization to Labor and Industries within 8 hours. OSHA
requires employers to report an employee amputation or loss of an eye within 24
4. Substantial loss related to agency policies, procedures, or management practices,
particularly where it appears there is a risk the event may recur.
5. Substantial loss related to litigation or defense practices.
6. Any breach (incident) of Information Technology (IT) Security. Refer to IT
Employee Report incident as soon as possible but no later than the following work •
day, to supervisor, Risk Manager, and/or Chief Information Security
Officer (CISO) depending on nature of incident. Refer to IT Division
Report must include information about what, when and where the •
Supervisor Provide assistance to ensure the person or area is safe. •
Risk Complete an Office of Risk Management (ORM) Loss History Incident •
CISO /incidentform.pdf and send to ORM along with supporting documents
If the person wants to file a claim against the state, direct them to the •
Ensure all reports and responses are in accordance with agency policy. •
Department Policy No. SAF-609-17 Page 3 of 3
Department Receive ORM Loss History Incident Report Form.
Per the ORM Incident Reporting Procedure (found at •
/guidelines.pdf), the Director of DES will decide if a special review
team should be convened to review the incident (or incident trend) in
depth. If so, a loss prevention review team (LPRT) will be assigned.
When the review is complete, the LPRT will provide a written report to •
the involved agency and the DES Director. The report and the agency
response will be on the DES Web site at LPRT Reports. (Most reviews
of reported incidents will not result in an LPRT. For these incidents,
the agency is notified that a review will not take place.)
Agency Review written report from LRPT (if applicable). •
Determine appropriate mitigation or corrective action measures. •
Notify manager about status of incident and if mitigation and/or •
Work with Risk Manager and/or CISO on mitigation measures. •
Work with Human Resource Director on disciplinary action. •
Confidential Data Loss. The action or state of not maintaining or having control over
confidential records in either paper or electronic form.
Incident. An event that may result in harm or loss and poses a risk to an agency goal,
become litigious, result in external complaints;
Information Technology Security Incident. Any unplanned or suspected event that
could pose a threat to the integrity or availability of confidential data or systems.
Potential Compromise of Agency Reputation. Incident or situation that could cause
damage to the agency’s reputation or credibility.
Significant Bodily Injury. Injury which creates a temporary loss of the function of any
bodily member or organ or temporary loss of any one of the five senses.
Substantial Property Loss (in excess of $100,000). Loss that is large in size, value, or
